domain: A glossary of terms from the provided text.

glossary.definition: It is considered that artificial intelligence should be viewed in close connection with our understanding what constitutes general intellect, and it means we cannot define AI without a standard to use for evaluating whether or not computer systems possess the characteristic. Historically however there has been an association of Artificial Intelligence as linked together withe human Intellect.
glossary.sentence: Rezultātā, ja mēs nevaram definēt, kas ir intelekts tad nemūsu var am arī definet kās  ir mākslīgais inteliģence,

glossary.definition: It is understood that intellect resides in humans and animals as well plants.
glossary.sentence: Šajā tēmā Jūs noskaidrosiet, ko mēs saprotam ar intelektu,

glossary.definition: A science branch where a range of theories about the nature and characteristics have been developed concerning human intellect.
glossary.sentence: taču tieši psihologijā ir izstrādāta virkne teoriju par cilvēka intelekts būtību

glossary.definition: Spearman considered the most important aspect of intelligence to be its unity, which he defined as general intellect.
glossary.sentence: Spīrmens (Spearmen) uzskatīja ka intelekta svarīgākā aspekts ir tā vienotība ko viņš definēja kā vispārējais inteliģence

glossary.definition: Thurstone identified seven primary mental abilities, which he considered equally important.

glossary.sentence: dzīvnieku dažādība ir vēl lielākaņemot vēra viņu kgnitivu spēju
tie mācās pamatojoties uz atkārtojos praksim
jābalstās uz fundamentālajiem principa kas nemainīsies laika gait

glossary.definition: The first definition of artificial intelligence was offered by John McCarthy in 1956.
bioloģiski iedvesmotas skaitļošanas struktūras.

glossary.sentence: neironu tīkli intelekta izpēte un valodu imiatcija.

glossary.definition: An approach that attempts to achieve an intellectual level, when the system is able to resolve broad ranges and new problems. (Russell un Norvig), or spēja risināt vispārīgas problēmas neatkarīgi no problēmsféras lĩdzi̇ti kā to spej daarït cilvēks;  spēj izmantot kopā visǔpašās, specifiskas specijás vienotaa veida. (Pennachin and Goertzel)
glossary.sentence: Tas paredz tādu datorsistēmu…kontrolēti sevi autonomi ar pašu domām.

glossary.definition: Šīs kategorijas akcentē uzvedību (3. att.).
kategorijas pa labi balstās uz ideālu intelektu, ko sauc par saprātīgu (4. att.).
Šis ir arī viens no fundamentālajiem testiemi mākslīgā intelekta jomă, lai noteiktu vai datorsistēmai piemīt vai nepiemit intellekts.

Aǵenti mācās bez cilvēka iejaukšanās, piemēram... aģentam ir jāizlemj… (Russell & Norvig, 2010).; No context it is understood as an entity that learns without human intervention and makes decisions based on rewards.
glossary.sentence: Aǵents mācās bez cilvēka iejaukšanäs...

glossary.definition: spēja apstrādāt dabiḡo valdodu, lai sekmīgi uzturētu sarunu ar testētāju
spēja veikt automātisko spiešanu, lai izmantotu glabato informaciju

lai uztvertu objektos.
lai pārvietotu objketus.

glossary.term: augstākā līmeņa programmējamo valodās rakstītas programmas
glossary.definition: Šīs izmanto atšķirīga simbolus un strukturālām struktūrēm, lai realizētu pamata procedūras.
Dators iekšēji atspoguļo pasauli izmantojot simbolikas strukturu.
Apgalvojums, kas nav pietiekams atdarināt cilvēka intelektu.
Slavens kritikas piemērs. Fiziska sistema darbojas ar fiziskajiem simboli, bet nepiemit intelekts.
Sistēmā nav intelektuāla darbība.
Norāda, kā reaģēt uz ieejas simboliem.
Pamatā gandrīz visiem praktiskajām un teorētiskais pētiņumiem.
...secīgi pievienojot, dzēšot vai mainjot zinâšanās (likumus)... produkciju sistēmās izveduma mehānisms katrā iterācijas pārskata visus znânšanu bází esoos likums.

imitē cilvēka smadzeņu neironus
Atspoguļo savienojumu spēku

uzsverot to galvenā atšķirības

The ability to solve general problems regardless of the problem area, similar as humans can do; The capability  to resolve issues in specific areas and contexts with particular efficiency. (Pennachin un Goertzel)
glossary.sentence: Vispārīgais mākslīgajam intelektam ir definētas šādas nepieciešamas īpašiības

glossary.definition: Intelligence that surpasses human capabilities (Russell and Norvig) and is therefore wiser than any of the minds in every field, including scientific creativity.
Focuses on a broader set organisms processes and phenomena across different spatial an temporal scales (Floreano un Mattiussi)
Used to perceive the environment.
used for system operation in an enviroment
When interacting with the real environment, they collectively achieve more complex behavior.
arises from interactions and cooperation of many less intellectual agents (Brownlee)
uses general methods, which can be applied to a wide range problems without using knowledge about the problem
(From text) Not defined directly but implied as one approach to creating intelligent computer systems.
Not Defined, mentioned in contrast with Symbolic
(From text) A field of study that includes the development and creation intelligent computer systems.
(from Text): Not defined directly, but refers to a form or type AI.
(from Text): Storage for data required when developing a system.
(from Text): A field where intelligent computer systems can be developed.
(From text)  A mathematical description used to represent real-world objects and their behavior in a way that is easy for calculations.
(from Text): Used as an example of how to describe robot arm movements.
(from Text): Identifying the current state, desired goal and difference between those states.
(from Text): The desired state or goal.
(from Text): Criteria used to select a specific action from available alternatives.
(from Text): One of the transport options considered for solving a problem.
mūsu fizisko pozīcijas vai atrašanās vietu, kas mainas braucot no A punkta uz B punktu. (brauciens)
konkrētā objekta vai parādības īpašību vērtība kopums noteiktajā laika brīdī.
formāls atspoguļojums problēmrisināšanas procesam

nulles summas, deterministiskas div personu spele ar pilnu informaciju.

glossary.definition: kur ir tris vinam atbilstosas figuras horizontali, diagonāli vai vertikáli
ieraksta savás figuras tukšajās rūtiņīs.
Vertices in the state space graph representing states within problem-solving process.
Edges that connect vertices within an adjancency graph.
A set of principles that selects the next vertex to be viewed from a collection of open vertices, such as selecting Vertex C2.

...izvēlas nākamo apskatāmo verstotu no atvērto virsotņu kopas...
...izvēlas nākamo apskatāmo verstotu no atvērto virsotņu kopas, pieņemš tas izvelēs versote C2. Kad ir izvēlata apstātam vertes...
...izvērš toģenerējot pētetečus.
Šīs darbības rezultātā versota C2 pārvēršas par slētu verstoti, bet atvētoto virstotņu kopu veido trīss vertotes: B1...

...meklějod garaako vai isākto va visas virstotes apmēklešošo ceļu (minimaals karakasu)...

...tika salīdzinātas nosacītās robata atrašanā koordinates, piemēram C2≠B3.

glossary.term: stāvokļu telpas grafa struktūras īpatnības(state space graph structure characteristics)

glossary.definition: A representation of all possible states and transitions in a problem-solving context.

glossary.term: pirmmeklešanas mērķa vīrzote( search target vertix)

glossary.sentence: Tā kā pārmeklešanas mērķa virsotne joprojām nav atrasta, tad pirmmeklējuma algoritms turpināt pētīt ceļus

glossary.definition: Algorithms that are used for searching, which in general terms divide into two categories (1. att.). - Search algorithms divided based on whether they have access to information about the quality of vertices or not.
A search algorithm which doesn't differentiate between the quality of verticies and simply explores nodes sequencially.
An algortihm that goes as deep into an adjancency graph before exploring other branches.
An algortihm that searches for the best state.
A list containing the vertices to be explored, as part of a search algorithm's process.

glossary.sentence: Contains closed vertices, which means they were already expanded.

glossary.definition: ģenerējot un izvērtějos tās pęctecus / priekštečus (generating and evaluating its successors/predecessors) - nodes that can be reached from a given node.
A vertex that has no successors.
Data structure working on the principle “Last In, First Out”.
The target vertex being sought in a search.
The root of a tree-like graph.
Branches in the adjancency list, which are explored by an algortihmic step.
A list of the next set to be searched.
Datu objektu attiecina tikai uz vienm klasteri un soļiem atkārtojas, līdz centroīdu vērtības nemainās. Ir arī iespējams pātrāukt procesa sasniedzot iepriekš noteiktu iteraciju skaitu.
A graph representing the possible states of problem space.
A search algorithm that explores a branch as far as it can before backtracking.

glossary.sentence: When the search algorithm can take a long time to find target vertex.
The level at which a node is situated. (ņem vērā līmeni kur ir izvietota)

glossary.definition: A data structure that follows the First-In, Last Out (FILO) principle.
Virsotne bez pievienotiem vai papildinātiem meklējami stāvokļiem. (Vertex without added or supplemented search states.)
Saraksts, kurā atrodas virsotnes vēl nav apskatītas un izvērstas.
Stavokļu telpas grafā virsotņu attālums no sākuma vertices. (The distance of vertices from the starting vertex in a state space graph.)

glossary.sentence: Tās kā tā nav pārmeklēšanas virsotne, tiek uzskatīta nākamā iterācijas.

glossary.definition: Savienojums starp divām virsotēm. (Connection between two vertices.)
Maksimālais dziļu, cik tālu algoritms izmeklē stāvokļa telpas grafu. (The maximum depth to which the algorithm explores a state space graph.)
Tā nav pārmeklēšanas mērķa virsotne, un tiek apskatīta algoritma darbības laikā. Atrodas sarakstiem OPEN vai CLOSED.
Tāda ir atbilstoša virsotine, kas tiek mekleta.
Šis dziņumā nosaka maksimālo attālumu no sākumpunkts līdz mērķim.
glossary.sentence: Tās virsotnes, kuru dzilumos ir vienads ar uzdoto diļuma ierobežojumiem.
Šī ir modifikācija, kas novērsm liela skaitļošanas resursū patereņa problēmām.

glossary.definition: A point in the search space or graph representing a state of problem solving. Šāda virsotne ir virsote E, līdz ar to ir atrasta saskares virsøtine.
Algorithms that cannot differentiate between paths leading to the search goal and those not. Tie stāvokļu telpas grafā nespēj atšķirt ceļus, kas ļauj sasniegt pārmeklēšanās mērķa virsøtni.
Algorithms that use heuristic knowledge to improve efficiency. Pārmeklēšanās mērķa virsøtnes atrašans efektivitāti var uzlabot, izmantojat heiristiksi infomrētus pārsmelešanuas algoritm.
An example of heuristic knowldedge. Mūsdienās plaši izmantojamais vārdu salikums “Life Hack” atbilst tam, ko saprot ar heiristiskām zināšanīem.
A field of study that often contrasts heuristic knowledge with methods guaranteeing a precise solution.

glossary.term: stāvokļu telpas grafā (in state space graph)
heiristiskas zinasanu izmantosanas piemērs(example of using heuristic knowledge)
glossary.definition: A common example is restarting a computer when it malfunctions.

A polynomial-like expression of 'n' factors, used to estimate the distance from a given state in problem solving. It is expressed as f(x) = ai * xi where n represents vertex;  ai refers i’s weight coefficient and x1 indicates heuristic estimation for factor ‘i’, which characterizes states quality.
glossary.sentence: kur n- virsotne; ai - i tā faktora svara koeficients ;xi – i tă fakta, kas raksturo problēmsrisināšanas stāvokļu kvalitāti heiristisks vērtējums

glossary.definition: The burden needed for a deep analysis of problems, which is reduced through function usage.
funkcijas, kas nav pietiekami labi definēt (heuristic evaluation function that is not sufficiently well-defined) - a method for estimating the cost or distance to reach from current state.
The target vertex that the search algorithm is trying to reach.
The distance from the starting vertex to a given vertix n.
A heuristic measure that characterizes quality of vertext or estimate  distance between vertec and target.
Characteristics that describe problem-solving states and are used in the heuristic evaluation function.
A point within a graph representing state of game or puzzle
A representation of all possible states and transitions in a problem-solving context. It can be very large.
A particular configuration or arrangement in a problem-solving context.
a board game involving two players

A specific configuration or arrangement in a problem-solving context.
When an AI system fails to see vertices beyond its defined depth.

Problems where a plan of action is created and represented as state space graph.

(L) The length of the longest path within state space graph.
(T),  the total number vertices to be searched in a stae spce grahp

Nodes that are either successors or predecessors in relation direction
y=m*x +b, where 'x' is an input variable value; ‘y’ represents output
neviena virsotne vēl netika izvērsta (no vertex has yet been expanded) - a list containing the nodes that have already been explored and are not part of current search path.

ļauj algoritam... (allows the algorithm to...) - a process or set of rules for solving problems, specifically enabling switching between different states and maintaining solutions even with imperfect heuristic evaluations.

glossary.definition: the destination or goal node within an environment being searched.
A graph representing the possible states in a problem-solving process, as mentioned when describing algorithms finding target nodes.
Function used to estimate distance or cost from current state towards goal. Its quality impacts algorithm efficiency.
Algorithms that use heuristic information (estimates) guide search process.
A compromise between hill climbing and best-state searching, selecting a limited number of 'best' nodes at each iteration.
Search strategy focusing on only the single ‘better’ node.
A single execution cycle within an algorithm.
Number determining how many best nodes are considered per iteration.
A node that is not the target, and leads to a dead end.
Estimates of distance or cost from current state towards goal.

glossary.sentence: A search process guided by data.

glossary.definition: The act of examining a vertex and generating its successors.

glossary.sentence: Distinct vertices, without duplicates.

glossary.definition: Guidelines or principles used to make decisions.

glossary.sentence: The ability of an algorithm perform effectively.

glossary.definition: Algorithms used in two-player games for searching, processing the game tree so that a computer can make moves. (Tāpēc spēlēm realizācijas tiek izmantots... lai dators varētu  izviedīt gājienus un virzēt speeli uz priekšu.)

*A player whose strategy is to prevent the maximizer from winning.* (šī spēlētāja nosacītā stratēģija ir darit visu iespējamo, lai neļautu maksimizetaajam uzvarētu)
